articles.headline: 2026 World Cup: Which 48 teams qualified for the tournament?
articles.description: The race to qualify for the 2026 FIFA World Cup is complete. Here's how it all went down across the globe.

images.name: Sweden celebrate [1296x729]
images.credit: Linnea Rheborg - UEFA/UEFA via Getty Images

images.name: Scotland celebrate [1296x729]
images.credit: Alan Harvey/SNS Group via Getty Images

images.name: World Cup trophy New York [1296x729]
images.credit: Mike Stobe - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images

images.name: Marcotti: Italy have 'no excuses' for another World Cup failure
images.caption: Gab Marcotti reacts to Italy failing to qualify for a third straight World Cup after losing to Bosnia and Herzegovina on penalties.

articles.headline: Could Morocco beat Brazil in Group C at the World Cup?
articles.description: Sam Ogunleye and Ed Dove discuss the strength of Morocco's squad as well as the potential to defeat group favourites Brazil.

articles.headline: New Adidas World Cup jerseys rated: Argentina, Mexico, Spain
articles.description: Adidas has released a huge batch of home kits for 22 countries, many of whom have either qualified or still hope to qualify for the FIFA 2026 World Cup.

article.headline: Bolivia thrash Venezuela in high-altitude World Cup qualifier
article.description: Bolivia claimed their second win in the South American World Cup qualifiers on Thursday with a 4-0 home victory over Venezuela on their first match at one of the world's highest altitude stadiums.
article.linkText: Bolivia thrash Venezuela in high-altitude qualifier

images.name: Bolivia celeb vs. Venezuela [1296x729]
images.alt: Enzo Monteiro of Bolivia celebrates after scoring the team's fourth goal during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Qualifier match between Bolivia and Venezuela at Estadio Municipal De El Alto on September 05, 2024 in El Alto, Bolivia.

peers.name: Bolivia celeb vs. Venezuela [1296x518]

article.story:
Bolivia claimed their second win in the South American World Cup qualifiers on Thursday with a 4-0 home victory over Venezuela on their first match at one of the world's highest altitude stadiums.

Some 20,000 fans gathered at the Municipal de El Alto stadium at 4,150 metres above sea level and 550 metres above the Hernando Siles stadium in La Paz, where Bolivia usually host international games.

"La Verde" made the most of their home advantage to earn newly-appointed coach Oscar Villegas his first triumph at the helm in a match that broke the altitude record for an international fixture.

Ramiro Vaca opened the scoring for the hosts in the 13th minute with a long-range shot that sailed into the top corner before Carmelo Algarañaz doubled the lead from the spot before the break.

Miguel Terceros found the net early in the second half and Enzo Monteiro in the 89th to seal the win that moved Bolivia provisionally into international playoffs spot in seventh place with six points, just one behind Brazil.

Bolivia next play at Chile on Tuesday, while fourth-placed Venezuela host Uruguay.

keyEvents.text: First Half begins.
Héctor Cuellar (Bolivia) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal! Bolivia 1, Venezuela 0. Ramiro Vaca (Bolivia) right footed shot from outside the box to the top left corner. Assisted by Robson Matheus.
Ramiro Vaca (Bolivia) is shown the yellow card for excessive celebration.
Substitution, Bolivia. Miguel Terceros replaces Henry Vaca because of an injury.
Goal! Bolivia 2, Venezuela 0. Carmelo Algarañaz (Bolivia) converts the penalty with a left footed shot to the bottom left corner.
Second Half begins Bolivia 2, Venezuela 0.
Substitution, Bolivia. José Sagredo replaces Lucas Chávez.
Substitution, Venezuela. Darwin Machís replaces Jhon Murillo.
Substitution, Venezuela. Yeferson Soteldo replaces José Martínez.
Goal! Bolivia 3, Venezuela 0. Miguel Terceros (Bolivia) left footed shot from very close range to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Roberto Fernández.
Substitution, Venezuela. Christian Makoun replaces Nahuel Ferraresi.
Substitution, Venezuela. Cristian Cásseres replaces Daniel Pereira.
Substitution, Venezuela. Kervin Andrade replaces Jhonder Cádiz.
Substitution, Bolivia. Luis Paz replaces Roberto Fernández.
Substitution, Bolivia. Enzo Monteiro replaces Carmelo Algarañaz.
Cristian Cásseres (Venezuela) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Yeferson Soteldo (Venezuela) is shown the yellow card.
Substitution, Bolivia. Daniel Camacho replaces Robson Matheus.
Goal! Bolivia 4, Venezuela 0. Enzo Monteiro (Bolivia) right footed shot from very close range to the top right corner. Assisted by José Sagredo.
Enzo Monteiro (Bolivia) is shown the yellow card for excessive celebration.
